things.name: Hans Christian Andersen Museum
things.description: Spectacular new museum opened in 2021 on the birthplace of Hans Christian Andersen; an immersive journey through the fairy-tale world of The Little Mermaid, The Ugly Duckling and Thumbelina

things.name: Hans Christian Andersen Birthplace
things.description: The modest house where Hans Christian Andersen was born in 1805; preserved as a museum within the larger Hans Christian Andersen quarter of Odense's historic centre

things.name: Odense Cathedral (St. Canute's Cathedral)
things.description: Gothic cathedral from the 13th-15th centuries housing the shrine of St. Canute, Denmark's royal martyr; the crypt contains the saint's preserved remains and is a national pilgrimage site

things.name: Funen Village Open Air Museum
things.description: Living history open-air museum with 25 buildings from the 18th and 19th centuries; costumed interpreters demonstrate traditional rural life on the island of Funen that inspired Andersen's fairy tales

things.name: Brandts Art Museum
things.description: Denmark's museum of art, photography and media in a converted textile factory; the outstanding collection covers Danish and international modern and contemporary art

things.name: Odense Old Town Walking Trail
things.description: Odense's historic pedestrian heart with half-timbered houses, cobbled streets and the Overgade and Nedergade district; one of the best-preserved medieval town centres in Denmark

things.name: Odense Zoo
things.description: Award-winning zoo in a riverside park on the Odense River with over 2,500 animals; consistently voted one of Scandinavia's best zoos with innovative naturalistic habitats

things.name: Carl Nielsen Museum
things.description: Museum dedicated to Denmark's greatest composer Carl Nielsen (1865-1931), also born near Odense; explores his life, music and relationship to the Funen landscape that shaped his work

things.name: Odense River Cruises
things.description: Guided boat tours along the Odense River from the city centre to the zoo and Funen Village; a pleasant way to explore the city from the water on traditional flat-bottomed boats

things.name: Nature Energy Park
things.description: Modern 15,790-seat stadium home of Odense Boldklub (OB), one of Denmark's most successful clubs with three league titles; the club has represented Denmark in European competition including the Europa League
